other_observers: The Denver Field Ornithologist field trip also saw this bird, including trip leader Ira Sanders.; No. Previously reported by many other observers including Brandon Percival, who may have initially found this bird.
specific_location: South Marina tires at Pueblo Reservoir.
description: See photo at http://www.flickr.com/photos/pauljhurtado/6411953279/in/photostream/  The gull was very large relative to nearby Herring and Ring-billed gulls, much too large for other dark backed gulls that could occur in Colorado including rarities like Western and Slaty-backed Gulls. Very dark mantle (almost as black as the primaries, which were tipped white), pink legs, heavy yellow bill with a small bit of red and black near the tip as is typical for the species. Head appeared clean white. Size, mantle color, leg color rule out other dark mantled gull species that have occured in or near Colorado including Kelp, Western and Slaty-backed Gull.Behaviors: Resting on the tires just out from the marina.Call: nonePlumage: winter adult
